attributes.description: Alerts when stream-based Lambda consumers show high IteratorAge, indicating the function is falling behind the record arrival rate.
attributes.name: [AWS Lambda OTel] High iterator age

investigation_guide.blob:
## High Iterator Age

### Triage Steps
1. IteratorAge applies to stream/poll-based sources (Kinesis, DynamoDB Streams, Kafka).
2. Check whether invocation errors or throttling are slowing processing.
3. Review function duration — slow handlers reduce throughput per shard.
4. Evaluate batch size and parallelization factor configuration.
5. Compare incoming record rate (`Invocations`) with processing capacity.

### Escalation
Growing iterator age means data freshness is degrading — prioritize before backlog becomes unrecoverable.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.lambda.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/Lambda"
    AND MetricName == "IteratorAge"
    AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS max_iterator_age_ms = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/Lambda/IteratorAge`)
  BY FunctionName, cloud.region
// Backlog age threshold (ms): stream consumer falling behind
| WHERE max_iterator_age_ms > 60000
| EVAL function_id = CONCAT(FunctionName, "/", cloud.region)
| SORT max_iterator_age_ms DESC
| LIMIT 10
